channel.title: Provenance - Free iOS & tvOS Retro Game Emulator
channel.description: Recent content on Provenance - Free iOS & tvOS Retro Game Emulator

item.title: How to Add Games to Provenance
item.description: Provenance supports games from 38+ retro systems, but you need to provide your own ROM files. This guide explains several methods for getting games into Provenance on iPhone, iPad, or Apple TV.

item.title: How to Install Custom Controller Skins
item.description: Provenance supports custom controller skins, letting you replace the default on-screen controls with artwork that more closely matches the original hardware. This guide walks you through finding, downloading, and installing custom skins.

item.title: How to Play on Apple TV with Provenance
item.description: Provenance is one of the only iOS emulators with native Apple TV support, letting you play 38+ retro game systems on your television. This guide covers both the native tvOS app and using AirPlay to mirror from your iPhone or iPad.

item.title: How to Use a Controller with Provenance
item.description: Provenance supports a wide range of physical game controllers on both iOS and tvOS, giving you a much more authentic retro gaming experience. This guide walks you through connecting a controller and getting it working in Provenance.

item.title: Provenance vs Delta: Which iOS Emulator Is Right for You?
item.description: Both Provenance and Delta are free options for playing retro games on iPhone. They serve different audiences, and the right choice depends on what systems you want to play and what devices you own. This page lays out the facts so you can decide for yourself.

item.title: Provenance vs ManicEmu: Which iOS Emulator Is Right for You?
item.description: ManicEmu is a newer iOS emulator that appeared on the App Store in 2024 alongside several other options following Apple’s policy changes. Provenance has been around much longer and takes a different approach in terms of scope, pricing, and platform support. Here is a factual look at how they compare.

item.title: Release 3.2.1
item.description:
Release 3.2.1 - iPad Skin Bug Fixes

Provenance 3.2.1 is a quick follow-up to the 3.2.0 release, addressing several skin-related bugs on iPad, joystick issues, and other fixes reported by the community.

item.title: Release 3.2.0
item.description:
Release 3.2.0

We’re excited to announce Provenance 3.2.0, a major update focused on performance, skins, and quality of life improvements. This release brings highly requested features and fixes that make Provenance faster and more enjoyable than ever.

item.title: Release 3.1.1
item.description:
Release 3.1.1 - Bug Fixes

Provenance 3.1.1 is a bug fix release focused on UI and settings improvements, fixing issues reported by the community after the 3.1.0 release.

item.title: Release 3.1.0
item.description:
Release 3.1.0 - Flycast, Dolphin, RetroAchievements

We’re excited to announce Provenance 3.1.0, a major feature release bringing Flycast (Dreamcast), Dolphin (GameCube/Wii), RetroAchievements support, and iCloud Sync to Provenance.

item.title: Release 3.0.4
item.description:
Release 3.0.4

Links

  • 📲 App Store
  • 📝 GitHub
  • 🏪 AltStore

Install Instructions

Download from the AppStore for iOS or macOS Catalyst!

item.title: Play Game Boy & Game Boy Color Games on iPhone | Provenance
item.description: Provenance supports both the original Game Boy and the Game Boy Color on iPhone, iPad, and Apple TV. With hundreds of classic titles spanning two generations — Pokemon Red and Blue, The Legend of Zelda: Link’s Awakening, Tetris, Pokemon Gold and Silver, Kirby’s Dream Land, and countless others — the Game Boy library is one of the most beloved in gaming history.

item.title: Play Game Boy Advance Games on iPhone & iPad | Provenance
item.description: Provenance includes a full Game Boy Advance emulator for iPhone, iPad, and Apple TV. The GBA had an outstanding lineup of games — Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen, The Legend of Zelda: The Minish Cap, Metroid Fusion, Castlevania: Aria of Sorrow — and Provenance lets you play the entire library on your Apple device.

item.title: Play Nintendo 64 Games on iPhone & iPad | Provenance
item.description: Provenance brings the Nintendo 64 to your iPhone, iPad, and Apple TV. The N64 delivered legendary 3D gaming experiences — Super Mario 64, The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time, GoldenEye 007, Mario Kart 64, and more — and Provenance makes it possible to revisit those games on your Apple device.

item.title: Play Nintendo DS Games on iPhone & iPad | Provenance
item.description: Provenance includes Nintendo DS emulation for iPhone, iPad, and Apple TV. The DS had a remarkable dual-screen library — Pokemon Diamond and Pearl, Mario Kart DS, The Legend of Zelda: Phantom Hourglass, Chrono Trigger DS, Professor Layton, and hundreds more — and Provenance brings that library to your Apple device.

item.title: Play PlayStation 1 Games on iPhone & iPad | Provenance
item.description: Provenance brings Sony’s original PlayStation to iPhone, iPad, and Apple TV. The PS1 era produced some of the most iconic games in history — Final Fantasy VII, Metal Gear Solid, Castlevania: Symphony of the Night, and hundreds more — and Provenance lets you play them all on your Apple device for free.

item.title: Play Sega Genesis Games on iPhone & iPad | Provenance
item.description: Provenance includes a Sega Genesis emulator for iPhone, iPad, and Apple TV, covering both the North American Genesis and the international Mega Drive. Sonic the Hedgehog, Streets of Rage, Mortal Kombat, Phantasy Star IV — the Genesis library is enormous and Provenance gives you access to all of it on your Apple device.

item.title: Play Super Nintendo Games on iPhone & iPad | Provenance
item.description: Provenance brings the Super Nintendo Entertainment System to your iPhone, iPad, and Apple TV. With a library spanning RPGs, platformers, fighting games, and more, the SNES remains one of the most beloved consoles ever made — and now you can experience it all on your Apple device without a jailbreak.

item.title: Release 2.2.0
item.description:
Links

  • 📲 iOS IPA
  • 📺 tvOS IPA
  • 📺 macOS APP
  • 📝 GitHub
  • 🏪 AltStore/SideStore
  • Installation Instructions

Screenshots

item.title: Release 2.1.1
item.description:
Release 2.1.1

Links

  • 📲 iOS IPA
  • 📺 tvOS IPA
  • 📝 GitHub
  • 🏪 AltStore
  • Installation Instructions

[2.1.1] - 2022-06-15

Added

  • Controls: PSX on-screen joystick can be disabled in settings. No longer shows when controller is connected
  • Swift UI beta for tvOS
  • tvOS theme support
  • Metal shader 200% speedup 👉 @mrjschulte
  • early dosbox testing code (no running yet)

Fixed

  • tgbdual crash on ios fixed

Updated

  • fceux update core to 2.6.2

item.title: Release 2.1.0
item.description:
Release 2.1.0

Links

  • 📲 iOS IPA
  • 📺 tvOS IPA
  • 📝 GitHub
  • 🏪 AltStore
  • Installation Instructions

Changes

Site

  • IPAs now hosted in Amazon S3 for faster downloads/installs.

Core options

  • Added support for more styles of Core Options (multiple select options, enumerations, Int and Float ranges)
  • New Options exposed for
    • Mupen (N64)
    • Mednafen
